15.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
15.kj: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
15.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

16.gl: properly, to touch, i.e. lay the hand upon (for any purpose; euphemistically, to lie with a woman); by implication, to reach (figuratively, to arrive, acquire); violently, to strike (punish, defeat, destroy, etc.)
16.kj: beat, ([idiom] be able to) bring (down), cast, come (nigh), draw near (nigh), get up, happen, join, near, plague, reach (up), smite, strike, touch.

16.gl: the lip (as a natural boundary); by implication, language; by analogy, a margin (of a vessel, water, cloth, etc.)
16.kj: band, bank, binding, border, brim, brink, edge, language, lip, prating, (sea-)shore, side, speech, talk, (vain) words.
16.dv: or (in dual and plural) שֶׂפֶת; probably from H5595 (סָפָה) or H8192 (שָׁפָה) through the idea of termination (compare H5490 (סוֹף));

17.gl: vigor, literally (force, in a good or a bad sense) or figuratively (capacity, means, produce); also (from its hardiness) a large lizard
17.kj: ability, able, chameleon, force, fruits, might, power(-ful), strength, substance, wealth.
17.dv: or (Daniel 11:6) כּוֹחַ; from an unused root meaning to be firm;

17.gl: to be able, literally (can, could) or morally (may, might)
17.kj: be able, any at all (ways), attain, can (away with, (-not)), could, endure, might, overcome, have power, prevail, still, suffer.

17.gl: a puff, i.e. wind, angry or vital breath, divine inspiration, intellect. or (concretely) an animal
